─[1]-Project──
lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Cursor Hook Output Limits ]─ ┐

name: cursor-hook-output-limits description: Cursor has 18+ hooks but only sessionStart additional_context can inject context — agent_message is silently ignored user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Grep

Cursor Hook Output Limits

Only 1 of 18+ Cursor hooks can inject context into the agent’s conversation. All others either gate/block, fire-and-forget, or have output silently discarded.

Context Injection Points

  1. sessionStart — additional_context (string injected into conversation). Fires once per explicit session (+ button). Does NOT fire for auto-opened initial session.

That’s it. There is no per-tool-use context injection in Cursor.

Confirmed Non-Working (Tested Feb 2026)

  • beforeShellExecution agent_message — documented in Cursor’s official docs and third-party tutorials but silently ignored on both allow and deny. The agent never sees the message. Tested with explicit canary words; confirmed not delivered.
  • beforeShellExecution user_message — silently ignored on allow. Only shown to the user on deny (as part of the rejection UI).
  • beforeSubmitPrompt additional_context — silently ignored. Only continue and user_message work.

Full Hook Surface (as of Cursor ~2.2)

HookFires onWhat worksContext injection
sessionStartNew session (+ button)additional_context, env, continue, user_messageYes — additional_context only
beforeSubmitPromptEvery promptcontinue, user_message (gate only)No
beforeReadFileFile readpermission, user_message (deny only)No
beforeShellExecutionShell commandpermission (allow/deny/ask)No — agent_message ignored
afterShellExecutionShell completeNoneNo
preToolUseBefore tool (matcher)decision, reason, updated_inputNo
postToolUseAfter toolupdated_mcp_tool_outputNo
afterFileEditFile writtenNoneNo
afterTabFileEditTab file editNoneNo
afterMCPExecutionMCP call doneNoneNo
postToolUseFailureTool failureNoneNo
preCompactBefore compactionNoneNo
postCompactAfter compactionNoneNo
afterCodebaseIndexedIndex doneNoneNo
beforeLintBefore lintNoneNo
afterLintAfter lintNoneNo
beforeBuildBefore buildNoneNo
afterBuildAfter buildNoneNo

Snags

  • agent_message does not work. Despite being in official docs, it is silently discarded on both allow and deny. Confirmed Feb 2026. File upstream bug.
  • beforeReadFile cannot rewrite file content — only allow/deny.
  • sessionStart does not fire for the auto-opened initial session. First-session context comes from .cursor/rules/*.mdc files only.
  • afterFileEdit and afterShellExecution output is completely discarded.
  • preToolUse supports a matcher field to filter by tool name (e.g., "matcher": "Write").

Lore’s Mitigation Strategy

With only sessionStart for context injection:

  1. .cursor/rules/lore-*.mdc — tiered static context via always-on and glob-matched rules. Works on every session including auto-opened first session.
  2. sessionStart additional_context — full dynamic banner (delegation, active work, knowledge map). Works on + sessions only.
  3. Fire-and-forget hooks (afterFileEdit, postToolUseFailure, preCompact) write state files for cross-session awareness but cannot surface nudges to the agent mid-session.
  4. beforeShellExecution — tracks bash count in state files. Cannot deliver nudges to the agent (agent_message broken). State is available for next sessionStart to read.
  5. preToolUse (Write) — blocks MEMORY.md writes. Gate-only, no context injection.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
